name: M-26-14 DM: Data Retirement — Gate 1 Approval Pending
description: Detects M-26-14 managed indices awaiting Gate 1 (pre-retirement) approval in the data retirement workflow. Fires when a retirement request has been in pending_gate1 state for more than 24 hours without approval, indicating a reviewer has not yet acted. The two-gate retirement workflow requires explicit human approval before any managed index can be scheduled for deletion. Unreviewed Gate 1 requests mean data is sitting in frozen tier indefinitely without a documented retention decision. M-26-14 §5(b) requires agencies to maintain auditable data lifecycle decisions for all managed log and asset data.
author: Elastic
query: FROM m2614-retirement-requests | WHERE state == "pending_gate1" | WHERE DATE_DIFF("hours", created_at, NOW()) > 24 | STATS pending_count = COUNT(*) BY index_name, created_at, phase_age_days, ilm_policy | SORT created_at ASC
note:
## Triage and Investigation

### Step 1 — Review the retirement candidates
- Query `m2614-retirement-requests` for `state: pending_gate1` to see all candidates.
- For each candidate index, confirm: age, ILM policy, business owner, and whether any active legal holds apply.

### Step 2 — Check for legal holds or investigations
- Review `m2614-retirement-requests` for any `legal_hold_initiated` entries covering the same index range.
- Consult with legal/compliance team for ongoing investigations.

### Step 3 — Approve or cancel
- To **approve Gate 1**: POST to `m2614-retirement-requests` with `{state: approved_gate1, index_name: <name>, gate1_approved_by: <your-username>, gate1_approved_at: <now>}`
- To **cancel retirement**: POST with `{state: cancelled, cancelled_by: <username>}`
- Approval triggers the Gate 1 Approval Watcher within 1 hour, which will switch the index to a delete-eligible ILM policy and open the Gate 2 case.

### Step 4 — Verify in Kibana Cases
- Find the associated Gate 1 case in Security > Cases and update with your decision.
- The two-gate audit trail is maintained in `m2614-retirement-requests`.
